text.title: Docker LLM Prompt Templates Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Prompts MUST be parameterized templates with named slots, not freeform prose; ad-hoc prompts rejected for the library.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Every prompt MUST specify the output shape (Dockerfile / Markdown table / JSON list) explicitly.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Generation prompts MUST embed safety baselines (non-root, HEALTHCHECK, no secrets in layers) into the instruction.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Each prompt MUST record the model + version it was tested against; "works on any LLM" claim rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Prompt library MUST be semver-versioned in a shared repo; copy-paste fragmentation rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Skip when LLM-driven Docker workflow not adopted OR one-off chat is sufficient.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.
